title: Method for augmenting a scene in real space with projected visual content
abstract: One variation of method includes: serving setup frames to a projector facing a scene; at a peripheral control module comprising a camera facing the scene, recording a set of images during projection of corresponding setup frames onto the scene by the projector and a baseline image depicting the scene in the field of view of the camera; calculating a pixel correspondence map based on the set of images and the setup frames; transforming the baseline image into a corrected image - depicting the scene in the field of view of the camera - based on the pixel correspondence map; linking visual assets to discrete regions in the corrected image; generating augmented reality frames depicting the visual assets aligned with these discrete regions; and serving the augmented reality frames to the projector to cast depictions of the visual assets onto surfaces, in the scene, corresponding to these discrete regions.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS I claim: 1. A method for augmenting a scene in real space with projected visual content comprising: â¢ serving a sequence of setup frames to a projector facing the scene; â¢ via a camera facing the scene: o recording a baseline image depicting the scene in a field of view of the camera; and o recording a set of scan images, each scan image in the set of scan images recorded during projection of a corresponding setup frame in the sequence of setup frames; â¢ calculating a pixel correspondence map based on pixel values in scan images in the set of scan images and pixel values in corresponding setup frames in the sequence of setup frames; â¢ transforming the baseline image into a corrected image based on the pixel correspondence map, the corrected image depicting the scene from a perspective of the projector; â¢ accessing associations between a set of animated visual assets and a set of discrete regions in the corrected image, each discrete region in the set of discrete regions in the corrected image depicting a discrete surface in the scene from the perspective of the projector; â¢ generating a sequence of augmented reality frames depicting the set of animated visual assets aligned with the set of discrete regions in the corrected image; and â¢ serving the sequence of augmented reality frames to the projector for projection onto the scene to cast animated depictions of the set of animated visual assets onto corresponding surfaces in the scene over a first period of time. 2. The method of Claim l: â¢ wherein serving the sequence of setup frames to the projector comprises, at a peripheral control module physically mounted to an exterior of the projector, sequentially serving the sequence of setup frames to the projector; â¢ wherein recording the set of scan images comprises, via the camera comprising a two-dimensional color camera integrated into the peripheral control module, 

 recording each scan image in the set of scan images responsive to serving a next setup frame in the sequence of setup frames to the projector; â¢ wherein accessing associations between the set of animated visual assets and the set of discrete regions in the corrected image comprises recording links between the set of animated visual assets and the set of discrete regions in the corrected image entered manually at a computing device coupled to the peripheral control module; â¢ further comprising storing the sequence of augmented reality frames in local memory in the peripheral control module; and â¢ wherein serving the sequence of augmented reality frames to the projector comprises, at the peripheral control module, serving the sequence of augmented reality frames from local memory to the projector when the computing device is disconnected from the peripheral control module. 3. The method of Claim 1: â¢ further comprising: o serving a first boundary mapping frame to the projector, the first boundary mapping frame depicting a sparse grid array of white dots over a black background; o recording a first boundary scan image during projection of the first boundary mapping frame by the projector; o serving a second boundary mapping frame to the projector, the second boundary mapping frame depicting an inverse of the first boundary mapping frame; o recording a second boundary scan image during projection of the second boundary mapping frame by the projector; o converting the first boundary mapping scan image and the second boundary scan image to black and white; o subtracting the second boundary scan image from the first boundary scan image to calculate a composite boundary scan image; o isolating a set of pixel clusters in the composite boundary scan image containing values greater than a threshold value; and o calculating a perimeter that encompasses the set of pixel clusters, the perimeter representing a boundary of the scene in a field of view of the projector; and 

â¢ wherein transforming the baseline image into the corrected image comprises transforming pixels, inside the perimeter, in the baseline image into the corrected image based on the pixel correspondence map. 4. The method of Claim 1, further comprising: â¢ detecting a contiguous group of horizontally-adjacent pixels in the pixel correspondence map containing a null x-component value; â¢ extracting a first x-component value from a first pixel immediately to the left of the contiguous group in the pixel correspondence map; â¢ extracting a second x-component value from a second pixel immediately to the right of the contiguous group in the pixel correspondence map; and â¢ writing nearest integer values, linearly interpolated across the contiguous group from the first x-component value to the second x-component value, to each pixel in the contiguous group of pixels in the pixel correspondence map. 5. The method of Claim 1: â¢ further comprising: o detecting a corpus of objects in the corrected image; and o highlighting the corpus of objects over the corrected image in a creator window at a computing device; and â¢ wherein accessing associations between the set of animated visual assets and the set of discrete regions in the corrected image comprises: o receiving selection of a first object, in the corpus of objects, in the corrected image; and o in response to receiving selection of a first animated visual asset, in a virtual library of animated visual assets at the creator window, linking the first animated visual asset to a first discrete region in the corrected image depicting the first object. 6. The method of Claim 1, further comprising: â¢ calculating a disparity map between the baseline image and the corrected image; â¢ distinguishing a set of discrete surfaces depicted in the corrected image based on depth discontinuities in the disparity map; and â¢ defining discrete regions, in the set of discrete regions, around discrete surfaces in the set of discrete surfaces. 7. The method of Claim 1: â¢ further comprising: o calculating a disparity map between the baseline image and the corrected image; o estimating a first depth value of a first object in the scene, relative to the projector, based on the disparity map; o isolating a first discrete region, in the set of discrete regions in the corrected image, depicting the first object; and o defining a first animation speed of a first animated visual asset, in the set of animated visual assets, assigned to the first discrete region in the corrected image proportional to the first depth value of the first object; and â¢ wherein generating the sequence of augmented reality frames comprises generating the sequence of augmented reality frames depicting the first animated visual asset animated at the first animation speed and aligned to the first object in the scene. 8. The method of Claim 1: â¢ wherein generating the sequence of augmented reality frames comprises pre rendering each augmented reality frame, in the sequence of augmented reality frames, at a computing device, the sequence of augmented reality frames depicting the set of animated visual assets animated across corresponding discrete regions in the corrected image; and â¢ wherein serving the sequence of augmented reality frames to the projector comprises streaming the sequence of augmented reality frames from the computing device to the projector during the first period of time. 9. The method of Claim 1, further comprising: â¢ following the first period of time, recording a verification image of the scene via the camera; â¢ calculating a warp, in a camera domain, that aligns corresponding features in the baseline image and the verification image; and â¢ in response to a magnitude of the warp exceeding a threshold magnitude: o transforming the warp from the camera domain to a projector domain based on the pixel correspondence map; 

 o applying the warp, in the projector domain, to the sequence of augmented reality frames to generate a sequence of adjusted augmented reality frames; and o following the first period of time, serving the sequence of adjusted augmented reality frames to the projector for projection onto the scene to cast depictions of the set of animated visual assets onto corresponding surfaces in the scene that shifted during the first period of time. 10. The method of Claim 9, wherein recording the verification image comprises recording the verification image in response to conclusion of the first period of time spanning a preset operation duration. 11. The method of Claim 1: â¢ further comprising: o detecting a corpus of objects in the corrected image; and o highlighting the corpus of objects over the corrected image in a creator window at a computing device; â¢ wherein accessing associations between the set of animated visual assets and the set of discrete regions in the corrected image comprises: o receiving selection of a first object, in the corpus of objects, in the corrected image; and o in response to receiving selection of a first animated visual asset, in a virtual library of animated visual assets at the creator window, linking the first animated visual asset to a first discrete region in the corrected image depicting the first object; â¢ further comprising: o following the first period of time, recording a verification image of the scene via the camera; o detecting the first object in the verification image; o in response to a change in position of the first object from the baseline image to the verification images exceeding a threshold change magnitude: â serving the sequence of setup frames to the projector; â via the camera, recording a second set of scan images, each scan image in the second set of scan images recorded during projection of a corresponding setup frame in the second sequence of setup frames; â calculating a second pixel correspondence map based on pixel values in each scan image in the second set of scan images and pixel values in corresponding setup frames in the second sequence of setup frames; â transforming the verification image into a second corrected image based on the second pixel correspondence map, the second corrected image depicting the scene from a second perspective of the projector; â detecting the set of discrete regions in the second corrected image; â generating a second sequence of augmented reality frames depicting the set of animated visual assets aligned with the set of discrete regions in the second corrected image; and â serving the second sequence of augmented reality frames to the projector for projection onto the scene over a second period of time. 12. The method of Claim l, further comprising: â¢ following the first period of time, recording a verification image of the scene via the camera; â¢ calculating a warp that aligns corresponding features in the baseline image and the verification image; and â¢ in response to a magnitude of the warp exceeding a threshold magnitude: o serving the sequence of setup frames to the projector; o via the camera: â recording a second set of scan images, each scan image in the second set of scan images recorded during projection of a corresponding setup frame in the second sequence of setup frames; and â recording a second baseline image depicting the scene; o calculating a second pixel correspondence map based on pixel values in each scan image in the second set of scan images and pixel values in corresponding setup frames in the second sequence of setup frames; o transforming the second baseline image into a second corrected image based on the second pixel correspondence map, the second corrected image depicting the scene from a second perspective of the projector; o detecting the set of discrete regions in the second corrected image; 

 o generating a second sequence of augmented reality frames depicting the set of animated visual assets aligned with the set of discrete regions in the second corrected image; and o serving the second sequence of augmented reality frames to the projector for projection onto the scene over a second period of time. 12. The method of Claim 12, wherein recording the verification image comprises recording the verification image in response to detecting motion of the camera greater than a threshold motion based on an output of a motion sensor coupled to the camera. 13. The method of Claim 1, further comprising: â¢ following the first period of time, recording a verification image of the scene via the camera; â¢ calculating a warp that aligns corresponding features in the baseline image and the verification image; and â¢ in response to a magnitude of the warp exceeding a threshold magnitude: o serving the sequence of setup frames to the projector; o via the camera, recording a second set of scan images, each scan image in the second set of scan images recorded during projection of a corresponding setup frame in the second sequence of setup frames; o calculating a second pixel correspondence map based on pixel values in each scan image in the second set of scan images and pixel values in corresponding setup frames in the second sequence of setup frames; o transforming the verification image into a second corrected image based on the second pixel correspondence map, the second corrected image depicting the scene from a second perspective of the projector; o detecting the set of discrete regions in the second corrected image; o generating a second sequence of augmented reality frames depicting the set of animated visual assets aligned with the set of discrete regions in the second corrected image; and o serving the second sequence of augmented reality frames to the projector for projection onto the scene over a second period of time. 14. The method of Claim 1, wherein generating the sequence of augmented reality frames comprises: â¢ at a computing device: o generating a set of background frames depicting a first subset of animated visual assets aligned with a first subset of discrete regions in the corrected image representing immutable surfaces in the scene; and o generating an asset map defining locations of a second subset of animated visual assets aligned with a second subset of discrete regions in the corrected image representing mutable surfaces in the scene; and â¢ at a peripheral control module comprising the camera: o storing the set of background frames, the asset map, and the second subset of animated visual assets in local memory; o generating overlay masks comprising the second subset of animated visual assets according to the asset map; and o overlaying overlay masks onto corresponding background frames, in the set of background frames, to generate the sequence of augmented reality frames. 15. The method of Claim 14: â¢ wherein generating the asset map comprises generating the asset map assigning a motion trigger to a first animated visual asset, in the second subset of animated visual assets, assigned to a first discrete region, in the second subset of discrete regions, in the corrected image; â¢ further comprising detecting motion proximal the scene based on an output of a motion sensor in the peripheral control module; â¢ wherein generating overlay masks comprises, in response to detecting motion proximal the scene, generating a first sequence of overlay masks depicting animation of the first animated visual asset within the first discrete region; â¢ wherein overlaying overlay masks onto corresponding background frames comprises overlaying the first sequence of overlay masks onto background frames, in the set of background frames, to generate a first sequence of augmented reality frames; and â¢ wherein serving the sequence of augmented reality frames to the projector comprises serving the first sequence of augmented reality frames to the projector in response to detecting motion proximal the scene. 16. The method of Claim 14, further comprising, following the first period of time: 

â¢ serving a second sequence of setup frames to the projector; â¢ via the camera: o recording a second set of scan images, each scan image in the second set of scan images recorded during projection of a corresponding setup frame in the second sequence of setup frames; and o recording a second baseline image depicting the scene in the field of view of the camera; â¢ calculating a second pixel correspondence map based on pixel values in scan images in the second set of scan images and pixel values in corresponding setup frames in the second sequence of setup frames; â¢ transforming the second baseline image into a second corrected image based on the second pixel correspondence map, the second corrected image depicting the scene from the perspective of the projector; â¢ warping the asset map to align the second subset of discrete regions assigned to the second subset of animated visual assets in the asset map with corresponding features detected in the second corrected image; â¢ generating a set of overlay masks comprising the second subset of animated visual assets according to the asset map; â¢ overlaying overlay masks, in the set of overlay masks, onto corresponding background frames, in the set of background frames, to generate a sequence of adjusted augmented reality frames; and â¢ serving the sequence of adjusted augmented reality frames to the projector for projection onto the scene to cast depictions of the set of animated visual assets onto corresponding mutable surfaces in the scene that shifted during the first period of time. 17. The method of Claim 16, wherein recording the second baseline image comprises recording the second baseline image in response to detecting motion proximal the scene based on an output of a motion sensor coupled to the camera. 18. A method for augmenting a scene in real space with projected visual content comprising: â¢ during a setup period: o serving a sequence of setup frames to a projector facing the scene; o via an optical sensor facing the scene: 

 recording a set of scan images, each scan image in the set of scan images recorded during projection of a corresponding setup frame in the sequence of setup frames; and recording a baseline image depicting the scene in a field of view of the optical sensor; â¢ calculating a pixel correspondence map based on pixel values in each scan image in the set of scan images and pixel values in corresponding setup frames in the sequence of setup frames; and â¢ transforming the baseline image into a corrected image based on the pixel correspondence map, the corrected image depicting the scene in the field of view of the optical sensor; â¢ accessing associations between a set of animated visual assets and a set of discrete regions in the corrected image; â¢ rendering a sequence of augmented reality frames depicting the set of animated visual assets aligned with the set of discrete regions in the corrected image; and â¢ during a scene augmentation period, serving the sequence of augmented reality frames to the projector for projection onto the scene to cast animated depictions of the set of animated visual assets onto a set of surfaces in the scene corresponding to the set of discrete regions in the corrected image. 19. The method of Claim 18: â¢ wherein serving the sequence of setup frames to the projector comprises, at a peripheral control module physically mounted to an exterior of the projector, sequentially serving the sequence of setup frames to the projector; â¢ wherein recording the set of scan images comprises, via the optical sensor comprising a two-dimensional color camera integrated into the peripheral control module, recording each scan image in the set of scan images responsive to serving a next setup frame in the sequence of setup frames to the projector; and â¢ further comprising rendering the corrected image in a creator window at a computing device communicatively coupled to the peripheral control module. 20. The method of Claim 19: â¢ further comprising storing the sequence of augmented reality frames in local memory in the peripheral control module; and 

â¢ wherein serving the sequence of augmented reality frames to the projector comprises, at the peripheral control module, serving the sequence of augmented reality frames from local memory in the peripheral control module to the projector during the scene augmentation period while the computing device is disconnected from the peripheral control module. 21. A method for augmenting a scene in real space with projected visual content comprising: â¢ serving a set of setup frames to a projector facing the scene; â¢ at an optical sensor facing the scene: o recording a set of images during projection of corresponding setup frames onto the scene by the projector; and o recording a baseline image depicting the scene in a field of view of the optical sensor; â¢ calculating a pixel correspondence map based on the set of images and the set of setup frames; â¢ transforming the baseline image into a corrected image depicting the scene based on the pixel correspondence map; â¢ accessing associations between a set of animated visual assets and a set of regions in the corrected image; â¢ generating a sequence of augmented reality frames depicting the set of animated visual assets aligned with associated regions in the set of regions in the corrected image; and â¢ serving the set of augmented reality frames to the projector for projection onto the scene to cast depictions of the set of animated visual assets onto surfaces in the scene corresponding to the set of regions in the corrected image.
